title: Dallas Cowboys 7-Round Mock Draft: Cowboys' 2024 Picks Revealed in Star-Telegram's Expert Review
body:
The Dallas Cowboys are gearing up for the highly anticipated NFL draft, set to take place in just a few weeks' time. As the team's front office finalizes their preparations, fans and analysts alike are eagerly speculating about the potential picks that will shape the future of America's Team. With eight selections at their disposal, the Cowboys have a wealth of opportunities to bolster their roster and address key areas of need.

One of the most pressing concerns for the Cowboys is strengthening their defensive line, an area that has struggled to find consistency in recent seasons. To address this, some pundits are tipping the team to target talented prospects such as LSU's Mansoor Delane, a dominant defensive tackle with the raw talent and physicality to make an immediate impact in the NFL. Delane's impressive collegiate career, marked by a string of standout performances and accolades, has cemented his status as one of the top defensive tackle prospects in the draft.

However, the Cowboys' draft strategy may not be solely focused on bolstering their defense. With a plethora of talented wide receivers set to enter the league, the team may also be tempted to select a playmaker to complement their existing receiving corps. USC's Kamari Ramsey is one such prospect, boasting a blistering combination of speed, agility, and ball skills that would make him an instant asset to any offense. Ramsey's impressive junior season, which saw him rack up over 1,000 receiving yards and 10 touchdowns, has caught the attention of scouts and analysts across the league.

As the draft approaches, the Cowboys will undoubtedly be weighing up a range of different options and scenarios. With eight picks at their disposal, they have the luxury of taking a more nuanced and balanced approach, potentially targeting a mix of immediate contributors and longer-term developmental prospects. The team's coaching staff will no doubt be pouring over game footage and scouting reports, searching for the perfect blend of talent, athleticism, and intangibles to propel their squad towards success.

One potential area of focus for the Cowboys may be the tight end position, where they currently lack a reliable and dominant presence. Enter prospects such as Washington State's Kyle Thompson, a versatile and athletic tight end with a rare combination of size, speed, and receiving skills. Thompson's impressive collegiate career, marked by a string of standout performances and a reputation as one of the top tight end prospects in the draft, makes him an attractive target for any team looking to upgrade their tight end corps.

As the NFL draft draws ever closer, the Dallas Cowboys will be facing a daunting array of decisions and challenges. With their eight picks at stake, the team's front office will need to demonstrate a shrewd and well-informed approach, leveraging their expertise and resources to identify and secure the top talent in the draft. As the Cowboys embark on this critical process, fans and analysts alike will be watching with bated breath, eager to see how their favorite team will shape their roster and forge a path towards success in the years to come.
